master_row.title: Elsasser Attractor Manifold
master_row.canonical_statement: The Elsasser attractor manifold is the regime in which magnetic and Coriolis influences balance and the system is driven toward a critical dimensionless state. In the Trawinistic reading, this balance is the natural dynamical endpoint of torque-driven rotation.
master_row.formation_note: Documented in the source as a scaling / order-of-magnitude relation relating ρ, μ, Ω, B.
master_row.dictionary: In the unification framework, a system evolves toward an attractor characterised by a dimensionless parameter ( 1), defined by ( = B^2,,^2), where (B) is magnetic field strength, () the mass density, () the permeability, and () the rotation rate.
master_row.encyclopedia: The Elsasser attractor occurs when magnetic and rotational scales enter near-balance, producing a self-organizing critical manifold. Interpretive role: This manifold is the organizing center for the rotation-scaling part of the canon. It explains why magnetic and rotational observables should collapse toward a common critical regime across widely different systems.
